books.title: John Brown’s Body
books.author: Stephen Vincent Benét
books.summary: The sweeping tragedy of the American Civil War is chronicled through the interwoven experiences of soldiers and civilians from both the North and the South.

books.title: Jibby Jones
books.author: Ellis Parker Butler
books.summary: Five boys who live near the Mississippi River embark on an adventure to find hidden pirate treasure.

books.title: The Autobiography of Calvin Coolidge
books.author: Calvin Coolidge
books.summary: The thirtieth president of the United States recounts his life story.

books.title: Eminent Victorians
books.author: Lytton Strachey
books.summary: Four witty and biting biographies of Victorians that take aim at the moral superiority of their subject’s time.

books.title: The Wonderful Adventures of Nils
books.author: Selma Lagerlöf
books.summary: Nils Holgersson, transformed to an elf, learns the meaning of kindness during his adventures with a flock of wild geese.

books.title: The Inspector General
books.author: Nikolai Gogol
books.summary: Corrupt officials in a small Russian town are thrown into disarray by the arrival of an Inspector General from the central government.

books.title: A Woman of No Importance
books.author: Oscar Wilde
books.summary: A mother’s secretive past clashes with high society when her son is offered an irresistible position with a charismatic nobleman.

books.title: The Worm Ouroboros
books.author: E. R. Eddison
books.summary: The King Gorice of Witchland and the lords of Demonland wage war in the high-fantasy lands of Mercury.

books.title: Satan’s Diary
books.author: Leonid Andreyev
books.summary: Satan’s visit to Earth in the form of an American billionaire forces him to reevaluate mankind.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: H. P. Lovecraft
books.summary: The complete U.S. public domain short fiction of horror legend H. P. Lovecraft.

books.title: The Mirror of the Sea
books.author: Joseph Conrad
books.summary: An autobiographical memoir on Joseph Conrad’s maritime experiences and his relationship with the sea.

books.title: The Red Thumbmark
books.author: R. Austin Freeman
books.summary: A detective struggles to prove the innocence of a man accused of stealing jewels, in the face of overwhelming evidence.

books.title: The Art of Money Getting
books.author: P. T. Barnum
books.summary: A short guide on achieving financial success.

books.title: Mary Olivier: A Life
books.author: May Sinclair
books.summary: A gifted young woman born into a strict upper middle class Victorian family struggles for decades to balance duty to her controlling mother with a driving need to learn and to create her art.

books.title: Olney Hymns
books.author: John Newton
books.summary: A collection of hymns written by John Newton and William Cowper.

books.title: Looking Backward
books.author: Edward Bellamy
books.summary: A man falls asleep in 1887 and wakes up over a century later in a socialist utopia.

books.title: In the Days of the Comet
books.author: H. G. Wells
books.summary: A strange comet collides with Earth, spreading vapors that change human consciousness in a variety of positive ways.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Robert E. Howard
books.summary: A collection of short fiction by Robert E. Howard, ordered by date of publication.

books.title: A Day at a Time
books.author: Archibald Alexander
books.summary: A 1916 Christian devotional reflecting on faith and morality during World War I.

books.title: Essays
books.author: Henry David Thoreau
books.summary: A collection of Henry David Thoreau’s essays.
